name: OpenAI Codex
description: OpenAI's coding agent spanning a cloud agent that runs tasks in isolated containers and an open-source CLI. Delegates parallel software tasks (features, fixes, PRs) powered by GPT-5.3-Codex, with network access disabled by default in the cloud.

evidence.value: Cloud agent completes real-world engineering tasks ending in tested, citable PRs
GPT-5-Codex (now GPT-5.3-Codex) substantially improved long-task completion and code quality

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Benchmark review and hands-on evaluation of PR-producing cloud tasks

evidence.value: Reliable shell, editor, and test-runner usage inside containers; setup scripts configure project dependencies

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Testing of in-container command execution, editing, and test running across repositories

evidence.value: Handles long-horizon tasks autonomously: reading codebases, implementing changes, running tests, and iterating until passing

multi_step_planning.methodology: Long-horizon task evaluation from issue description to passing tests and PR

evidence.value: AGENTS.md files persist project conventions and instructions across tasks; cloud tasks are otherwise stateless per container

memory_persistence.methodology: Review of AGENTS.md guidance persistence and per-task container statelessness

evidence.value: Iterates on failing tests and lint errors until passing; surfaces logs and citations when blocked

error_recovery.methodology: Observed recovery behavior from failing tests, build errors, and missing dependencies

evidence.value: Many tasks run in parallel containers simultaneously, though without inter-agent handoff primitives

agent_collaboration.methodology: Assessment of parallel task fan-out and coordination model

evidence.value: Cloud tasks run in isolated containers with internet access disabled by default during execution; configurable domain allowlists
CLI offers OS-level sandboxing (Seatbelt on macOS, Landlock/seccomp on Linux) with approval modes

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Review of container isolation, default-deny network policy, and CLI sandbox mechanisms

evidence.value: Scoped GitHub repo access, per-environment configuration, and CLI approval modes (suggest/auto-edit/full-auto)

access_control.methodology: Assessment of repository scoping, environment controls, and approval mode granularity

evidence.value: Default-disabled internet during execution sharply limits exfiltration from injected instructions; agent-specific safety training applied

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Review of network-isolation mitigations and model-level injection defenses

evidence.value: Each task gets its own ephemeral container preloaded only with the target repository and configured environment

data_isolation.methodology: Architecture review of per-task container isolation and environment scoping

evidence.value: Codex CLI is open source under Apache-2.0; the cloud agent service and models remain proprietary

open_source_transparency.methodology: License and source availability review of CLI versus cloud service

evidence.value: Business/Enterprise data excluded from training by default; consumer ChatGPT plan settings govern Codex task data

data_retention.methodology: Review of OpenAI retention and training policies across ChatGPT plan tiers

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II and DPA available for business tiers covering Codex usage

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance certification and DPA availability review

evidence.value: Repository code is processed by OpenAI; default-off internet prevents task-time data flows to other third parties

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis of repository access, GitHub integration, and network policy

evidence.value: Open-source CLI runs locally and supports OpenAI-compatible endpoints, but flagship Codex models require OpenAI's cloud

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment of local CLI versus cloud-only agent and models

evidence.value: Dedicated developer docs covering cloud environments, CLI, IDE integration, AGENTS.md, and pricing

documentation_quality.methodology: Documentation completeness review across cloud, CLI, and IDE surfaces

evidence.value: Tasks produce verifiable evidence: terminal logs, test outputs, and citations for every action taken

execution_traceability.methodology: Review of task logs, test output citations, and diff provenance

evidence.value: Agent explains its approach in task summaries with linked evidence before users accept changes

decision_explainability.methodology: Assessment of task summaries, cited reasoning, and pre-merge review surfaces

evidence.value: Apache-2.0 CLI with active public development; cloud agent and models closed

open_source_code.methodology: Open source assessment weighting open CLI against proprietary cloud service

evidence.value: Tens of thousands of stars, rapid release cadence, and a large contributor community since April 2025

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis via GitHub activity and release cadence

evidence.value: Available in ChatGPT (web/mobile), as a CLI, IDE extensions, and GitHub integration with @codex mentions

ease_of_integration.methodology: Setup and integration surface assessment across ChatGPT, CLI, IDE, and GitHub

evidence.value: Cloud architecture runs many tasks in parallel isolated containers, enabling fleet-style delegation

scalability.methodology: Assessment of parallel container execution and plan-tier task throughput

evidence.value: Included in ChatGPT plans with a $100/mo Pro 5x tier added 2026-04-09; typical usage estimated ~$100-200/dev/month

cost_predictability.methodology: Pricing model analysis of plan-based limits, Pro 5x tier, and typical-usage estimates

evidence.value: Per-task logs, usage dashboards, and admin controls for business plans; deeper APM requires external tooling

monitoring_capabilities.methodology: Review of task logs, usage visibility, and admin monitoring features

evidence.value: Research preview 2025-05-16, ChatGPT Plus rollout 2025-06-03, GPT-5-Codex upgrades Sept 2025; now mature on GPT-5.3-Codex

production_readiness.methodology: Maturity assessment from rollout timeline, model upgrades, and enterprise availability

code-generation.notes: Core use case: parallel feature work, bug fixes, refactors, and PR generation with test evidence

data-analysis.notes: Capable of scripted analysis within containers, though network-off defaults limit live data access

research-assistant.notes: Strong at codebase Q&A and architecture exploration; not aimed at general web research

education.notes: Cited logs and diffs make its work reviewable for learning, but it targets professional workflows

best_for: Teams delegating parallel coding tasks (fixes, features, PRs) to a cloud agent
Security-conscious organizations wanting network-isolated execution by default
ChatGPT Plus/Pro/Business subscribers seeking included coding-agent capacity
Developers wanting an open-source local CLI with OS-level sandboxing
strengths: Strong isolation: per-task containers with internet disabled by default during execution
Runs many tasks in parallel for fleet-style software delegation
Verifiable outputs with terminal logs, test results, and action citations
Open-source Apache-2.0 CLI with local OS-level sandboxing
Deep GitHub integration from task to reviewed pull request
limitations: Default network isolation can block tasks needing external dependencies unless allowlists are configured
Cloud agent and Codex models are proprietary with no self-hosted option
Plan-based limits are opaque; heavy users may need the $100/mo Pro 5x tier (~$100-200/dev/month typical)
Stateless per-task containers limit cross-task memory beyond AGENTS.md
Environment setup scripts add onboarding friction for complex monorepos

metadata.license: Cloud agent proprietary; Codex CLI Apache-2.0 (github.com/openai/codex)
metadata.deployment_type: Managed cloud containers + local open-source CLI
metadata.first_release: CLI April 2025; cloud agent research preview 2025-05-16; ChatGPT Plus rollout 2025-06-03
metadata.pricing: Included in ChatGPT plans; $100/mo Pro 5x tier (added 2026-04-09); typical usage ~$100-200/dev/month
